title: Maximum Element-Sum of a Complete Subset of Indices
description:
You are given a 1-indexed array nums. Your task is to select a complete subset from nums where every pair of selected indices multiplied is a perfect square,. i. e. if you select ai and aj, i * j must be a perfect square.
Return the sum of the complete subset with the maximum sum.
Example 1:
Example 2:
Constraints:

examples.example_text:
Input: nums = [8,7,3,5,7,2,4,9]
Output: 16
Explanation:
We select elements at indices 2 and 8 and 2 * 8 is a perfect square.

Input: nums = [8,10,3,8,1,13,7,9,4]
Output: 20
Explanation:
We select elements at indices 1, 4, and 9. 1 * 4 , 1 * 9 , 4 * 9 are perfect squares.

hints: Define P(x) as the product of primes p with odd exponents in x's factorization. Examples: For x = 18, factorization 21 × 32, P(18) = 2; for x = 45, factorization 32 × 51, P(45) = 5; for x = 50, factorization 21 × 52, P(50) = 2; for x = 210, factorization 21 × 31 × 51 × 71, P(210) = 210.
If P(i) = P(j), nums[i] and nums[j] can be grouped together.
Pick the group with the largest sum.
